title: euphorbia polycarpa var. intermixta
intro: Euphorbia polycarpa var. intermixta, also known as the desert wolf's milk or wolf's milk, is a succulent plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Euphorbia polycarpa var. intermixta include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's sap or latex is often used to create poultices or salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area for relief from pain and inflammation.
